# pycheckem

> pycheckem snapshots Python runtime environments and produces structured
> diffs to debug parity issues. It captures packages (with install sources
> via PEP 610), Python version, OS details, environment variables, sys.path,
> PATH, config file hashes, and project metadata — then compares any two
> snapshots to show exactly what's different.

## Quick Start

- Install: `pip install pycheckem`
- Snapshot: `pycheckem snapshot -o env.json --label prod`
- Diff: `pycheckem diff staging.json prod.json`
- Compare live vs saved: `pycheckem compare baseline.json`
- Guard in CI: `pycheckem guard baseline.json --fail-severity major`
- Verify deps: `pycheckem verify requirements.txt --exit-code`

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/MateuszMarciszewski/Pycheckem#readme): Full CLI reference, Python API, and examples
- [GitHub Actions Guide](https://github.com/MateuszMarciszewski/Pycheckem/blob/main/docs/github-actions.md): CI integration with baseline management
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/MateuszMarciszewski/Pycheckem/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/MateuszMarciszewski/Pycheckem/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute

## Key Features

- Zero external dependencies (stdlib only)
- Severity scoring: identical, minor, major, critical
- CI gating with `--exit-code` and `--fail-severity`
- PEP 610 install source detection (PyPI, editable, local, VCS, archive)
- Diff suppression via pyproject.toml config or CLI flags
- Four output formats: ascii, json, rich (color), side-by-side
- Snapshot history tracking
- Remote SSH snapshots
- Plugin system for custom collectors
- Dependency verification against requirements.txt and pyproject.toml
